title: Writing Favors
learningGoal: The learner will construct sentences expressing the giving and receiving of items and favors, accurately applying 'agemasu', 'moraimasu', and 'kuremasu' along with their respective particles.
instructions: Write four Japanese sentences based on the English prompts. Pay strict attention to who is the subject, who is the recipient, and whether you must use Agemasu, Moraimasu, or Kuremasu.

contentItems.text: Remember: You can never use 'Agemasu' if you are the one receiving the item or favor.

writingPrompt: Translate: 1) Tanaka gave me a book. 2) I helped my friend. 3) I had the teacher teach me English. 4) My friend lent me an umbrella.
promptGoal: To construct grammatically accurate sentences differentiating between kuremashita (they gave me), agemashita (I gave them), and moraimashita (I received).
referenceHints: Sentence 1: [Tanaka-san] wa/ga watashi ni [Book] wo [Gave me].
Sentence 4: [Friend] wa watashi ni [Umbrella] wo [Lend-Te] [Gave me].

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: たなかさんは わたしに ほんを くれました。わたしは ともだちを てつだって あげました。わたしは せんせいに えいごを おしえて もらいました。ともだちは わたしに かさを かして くれました。
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Tanaka-san wa watashi ni hon wo kuremashita. Watashi wa tomodachi wo tetsudatte agemashita. Watashi wa sensei ni Eigo wo oshiete moraimashita. Tomodachi wa watashi ni kasa wo kashite kuremashita.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: Tanaka gave me a book. I helped my friend. I had the teacher teach me English. My friend lent me an umbrella.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect selection of giving verbs based on the subject and direction of the action.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner write 'kuremashita' for Tanaka giving the book?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: たなかさんは わたしに ほんを くれました。わたしは ともだちを てつだって あげました。わたしは せんせいに えいごを おしえて もらいました。ともだちは わたしに かさを かして くれました。
acceptableAlternates: たなかさんが ほんを くれました。わたしは ともだちを てつだって あげました。わたしは せんせいに えいごを おしえて もらいました。ともだちが かさを かして くれました。
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Mastery of Kuremasu/Moraimasu/Agemasu combined with Te-forms.

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant writing! Your understanding of social directionality in Japanese is excellent.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review who the subject is. If 'I' am the subject and someone gave me something, use 'Moraimasu'. If 'THEY' are the subject and gave me something, use 'Kuremasu'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you didn't write 'Tanaka-san wa agemashita' for sentence 1. It MUST be kuremashita.

hintText: If the sentence starts with 'Watashi wa', you cannot use Kuremasu. You must use Moraimasu or Agemasu.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Sensei ni oshiete kuremashita'. (If 'Sensei ni' is used, the verb must be 'moraimashita').
